title: The Fifth Room
story_reference: Based on story-006-the-five-rooms.md, scene: the disciple walks into the fifth chamber and says 'yes' — and the Guru asks him who said it
verse_reference: Verses 154, 170 (Pancha Kosha — the five sheaths and the witness beyond them)
hook: Four rooms, four answers, four refusals. The fifth room was warm. He said yes. The Guru asked him who had just said it.

script.visual: Inside a ruined stone temple, late afternoon light cutting through cracks. Two barefoot figures moving through a narrow stone passage. The camera is behind them, low.
script.voiceover: Five chambers. Each smaller. Each darker. The Guru had said: 'Today you will meet yourself five times. And five times, you will be wrong.'

script.visual: Quick flashes — each held one second. Room one: a cracked mirror, a body. Room two: a draft moving dust, a breath. Room three: silence so thick the disciple's hand goes to his temple. Room four: a corridor so low both men stoop.
script.voiceover: Body. Breath. Mind. Intellect. Each time the old man asked: is this you? Each time the disciple said no, and each time he went deeper.

script.visual: A stone doorway. Warm light coming from inside. The disciple steps through. The camera stays with his face as he enters. His shoulders drop. His jaw unclenches.
script.voiceover: The fifth room was warm. Not sunlight warm. Not fire warm. A warmth that came from nowhere.

script.visual: The disciple stands in the fifth room. The warmth is almost visible — dust motes, soft gold light. His face changes. Something he has been chasing his whole life is here.
script.voiceover: He felt something he knew from deep sleep. From the moment after laughter. From watching a sunset so beautiful that thought simply stopped. Bliss. He wanted to stay.

script.visual: The Guru enters the frame, quiet. Asks softly.
Close on the disciple's face. The word comes from his marrow.
The Guru waits. The warmth pulses. He speaks barely above a whisper. The disciple's breath catches — the same catch as when he saw the rope was not a snake.
script.voiceover: The Guru waited. Then he said: 'The one who just said yes. Is he inside this room? Or is he the one who walked through all five?'

script.visual: The disciple does not answer. He does not reach for the bliss. He stands in the awareness that contains it — the way the sky contains a cloud without becoming wet. Cut to black.

cta: Scene six. The last scene of the series. What room are you in right now — and who is the one who knows the answer?
caption:
Body. Breath. Mind. Intellect. Bliss. The Vivekachudamani calls them the five sheaths — the five rooms you walk through when you ask what you are.

Most people stop at the third room and call it a self. The fourth is mistaken for the deepest. The fifth — bliss — is the one people chase their whole lives.

Then the Guru asks the question that ends the search. The one who said 'yes' in the bliss — was he the bliss? Or was he the one who walked through all five?

You are not the clouds. You are the sky that contains them.

sākṣin
music_suggestion: A single low drone opens and holds through rooms one through four. When the disciple enters the fifth room, the drone resolves into a single warm tone — the only moment of resolution in the entire reel series. When the Guru asks his question, the warm tone cuts abruptly to silence. The reel ends in silence. No swell. No bells. No final chord.
casting_notes: Two characters: The Seeker (the same actor across the whole six-reel series — by this scene he has visibly changed, quieter, less armor) and The Guru (minimal speech — three lines total in this reel). Location: a real ruined stone structure with multiple chambers — a fort, an old temple complex, a stone cellar. Each 'room' can be a different camera angle of the same space lit differently. The fifth room needs genuine warm directional light (golden hour through a window, or a practical lamp) — the warmth should be visible on skin. Alternative: the entire reel can be shot in close-ups inside a single stone chamber with five different lighting setups. Do not build a set. Use a real ruin.
